content.title: CompTIA Security+
content.summary: CompTIA Security+ is a vendor-neutral cybersecurity certification focused on practical security knowledge, threat awareness, and operational decision-making. It is positioned as an entry-to-intermediate credential for early security roles and as a gateway to more advanced CompTIA certifications.

credential_details.intended_audience: aspiring SOC analysts, junior security engineers, system administrators, and DevSecOps practitioners

knowledge_areas: General security concepts: CIA triad, security controls, risk concepts, and governance fundamentals.
Threats, vulnerabilities, and mitigations: malware, social engineering, threat actors, vulnerability types, and defensive responses.
Security architecture: secure network design, cloud and hybrid environments, virtualization, and Zero Trust concepts.
Security operations: monitoring, logging, incident response, digital forensics basics, and IAM operations.
Security program management and oversight: risk management, compliance, security policies, and third-party risk.
learning_outcomes: Analyze common cyber threats and map them to appropriate mitigations.
Reason about secure architectures across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments.
Apply incident response principles in realistic operational scenarios.
Explain authentication, authorization, and access-control flows clearly.
Connect technical controls to governance and business-risk requirements.
tools_and_frameworks: Core frameworks and concepts: CIA triad, defense in depth, IAM, incident response lifecycle, and NIST-style security thinking.
Tooling concepts: SIEM, IDS/IPS, firewalls, WAFs, IAM systems, and endpoint protection platforms.
Exposure type: operational reasoning and decision-making rather than product-specific configuration.
